title: iris vicaria
intro: Iris vicaria, also known as the gladdon or gladdon iris, is a plant species in the iris family that is native to Europe and Asia. It is a flowering plant with purple-blue flowers, typically found in damp meadows and along streams. The medicinal properties of Iris vicaria include its ability to be used as an anti-inflammatory and a diuretic, making it useful in treating conditions such as arthritis and urinary issues. The health benefits of Iris vicaria include its ability to reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory and gastrointestinal problems, and it can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or dried root to be used in herbal remedies.
